title: Generics, Type Sets, Constraints, Inference, and Iteration · Runtime Recall
summary: A collection library needs reusable operations without reflection, lost relationships, over-broad constraints, or unreadable APIs. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a constrained reusable collection library after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define generic functions and types, follow type inference, and retain relationships among parameters and results.
Build minimal constraints from method elements, unions, underlying-type approximations, comparable, and intersection semantics.
Use only operations guaranteed by the type set and explain why fields, methods, conversions, and nil differ across type arguments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
